file_insights.purpose: Abstract base controller with standardized response helpers for all API controllers

patterns_discovered.description: Standalone functions (successResponse, paginatedResponse, errorResponse) that create formatted API responses, complementing the BaseController class methods
Consistent response structure with success flag and data/error fields, supporting single items, paginated results, and error details
Methods map to specific HTTP status codes: success (200), created (201), noContent (204), and error responses follow REST conventions
Protected methods to extract organization, user, and request IDs from request context with validation and error handling
Helper methods for normalizing pagination (page, perPage) and sorting (sortBy, sortOrder) parameters with sensible defaults and constraints
Consistent metadata structure including page, perPage, total, totalPages, hasNextPage, and hasPrevPage flags

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Response vs Return Type in noContent
gotchas_discovered.description: noContent() directly calls res.status(204).send() and returns void, unlike other helpers that return response objects. This requires the res parameter and differs from the pattern of returning data.
normalizePagination enforces perPage maximum of 100 items and minimum of 1, which could silently change user-provided values. This is intentional for API protection but not immediately obvious.
normalizeSort accepts three different query formats (sort, sortBy, sortOrder) with fallback behavior. Order of precedence (sort > sortBy) may not be intuitive.
errorResponse casts error.details as a union type to extract errors array and retryAfter, relying on AppException structure that may not always match expectations.
getOrganizationId throws AppException if context is missing, but downstream services must handle this pattern. Multi-tenant requirement is strict but not enforced at type level.

approach_outcome.task_completion: Complete implementation of BaseController with comprehensive response helpers
approach_outcome.approach_used: Single-file creation with exported utility functions and abstract base class
approach_outcome.code_quality: High - includes extensive JSDoc comments, TypeScript interfaces, examples, and well-organized sections with clear separation of concerns
design_decisions: Provided both standalone response helper functions and class methods for flexibility
Created comprehensive type interfaces (ApiResponse, PaginatedApiResponse, ApiErrorResponse) for type safety
Included normalization helpers for pagination and sorting to ensure consistent API behavior
Implemented context extraction helpers to abstract RequestWithOrg interface usage
Added request validation and field validation helpers for request body validation

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding type constraints to normalizeSort using generics to ensure sortBy values match allowed fields at compile time
Add explicit documentation about the tenant-aware behavior of getOrganizationId and its role in multi-tenancy enforcement
Document the hard limits (perPage max 100) and defaults (page 1, perPage 20) to set clear expectations for API consumers
Consider adding a helper method to validate request body fields that returns ApiErrorResponse directly to reduce boilerplate in controllers
Create comprehensive unit tests for normalizePagination, normalizeSort, and buildLocation to catch edge cases in parameter handling
Ensure all controllers extending BaseController properly handle the difference between returned responses and direct res.send() patterns (noContent)
